clusters.summary: Seed, Pre Seed-focused fintech, consumer-internet, healthcare investors primarily based in NYC, with shared investments in Warby Parker, Plaid, Scopely
Series A, Growth-focused saas, ai, fintech investors primarily based in NYC, with shared investments in Ramp, Spotify, Openai
Series A, Seed-focused fintech, ai, saas investors primarily based in Other US, with shared investments in Solana, Wish, Pinterest
Seed, Growth-focused consumer-internet, healthcare, technology investors primarily based in Other US, with shared investments in Finch Therapeutics, Xero, Visa
Series A, Seed-focused fintech, saas, consumer-internet investors primarily based in Europe, with shared investments in Fuse Energy, Aspora, Dash0
Seed, Series A-focused consumer-internet, saas, fintech investors primarily based in LA, with shared investments in Spotify, Sandbox Vr, Sleeper
Seed, Series A-focused healthcare, fintech, climate investors primarily based in Boston, with shared investments in Infinite Cooling, Amperesand, Ninja Van
Pre Seed, Seed-focused fintech, ai, saas investors primarily based in Sf Bay Area, with shared investments in Mercury, Gusto, Doordash
Seed, Pre Seed-focused saas, consumer-internet, fintech investors primarily based in Other US, with shared investments in Ring, Civic, Kustomer
Seed, Pre Seed-focused cybersecurity, consumer-internet, fintech investors primarily based in Europe, with shared investments in Cyera, Vega, Savvy
Growth, Series B-focused fintech, saas, ai investors primarily based in Sf Bay Area, with shared investments in Slack, Airbnb, Robinhood
Seed, Series A-focused infrastructure, ai, developer-tools investors primarily based in Sf Bay Area, with shared investments in Launchdarkly, Tailscale, Pagerduty
Seed, Series A-focused ai, fintech, healthcare investors primarily based in Sf Bay Area, with shared investments in Applied Intuition, Coinbase, Ramp
Seed, Series A-focused ai, fintech, consumer-internet investors primarily based in Sf Bay Area, with shared investments in Uber, Decagon, Spacex
Seed, Series A-focused fintech, consumer-internet, saas investors primarily based in Sf Bay Area, with shared investments in Gusto, Highnote, Retool

curated_collections.name: The OGs
curated_collections.tagline: Investing since before the dot-com bubble burst. They've seen every cycle and are still writing checks.

curated_collections.name: The Original Super Angels
curated_collections.tagline: The 2005-2012 wave that proved individual investors could compete with big firms. They invented the angel-at-scale model.

curated_collections.name: The Hardest of Hard Tech
curated_collections.tagline: Atoms over bits. These investors back rockets, robots, chips, and defense systems where the moat is physics, not code.

curated_collections.name: Not Afraid of Regulators
curated_collections.tagline: They invest where others fear to tread — fintech, healthcare, crypto, insurance. Regulatory complexity is a moat, not a bug.

curated_collections.name: The AI True Believers
curated_collections.tagline: Invested in AI before it was cool — and doubled down after. Their portfolios include the companies building the foundation models.

curated_collections.name: Crypto Conviction
curated_collections.tagline: HODLing through every winter. They kept backing blockchain and crypto infrastructure when everyone else ran.

curated_collections.name: The Fintech Mafia
curated_collections.tagline: Deep in the financial plumbing. They backed multiple fintech unicorns and understand payments, lending, and banking infrastructure cold.

curated_collections.name: The Consumer Whisperers
curated_collections.tagline: They see consumer behavior shifts before anyone else. Portfolios full of the apps and marketplaces that changed daily life.

curated_collections.name: The Solo GPs
curated_collections.tagline: No partnership, no committee, no BS. They write their own checks and make their own decisions — often faster than anyone else.

curated_collections.name: The Unicorn Collectors
curated_collections.tagline: Five or more unicorns in a single portfolio. These investors have an uncanny ability to pick winners — or the dealflow to never miss.

curated_collections.name: The First Check Club
curated_collections.tagline: They wrote the seed check that started a unicorn — and did it more than once. The rarest skill in venture: picking winners before anyone else sees them.

curated_collections.name: The Hedge Fund Crossovers
curated_collections.tagline: Wall Street money that crossed over to Sand Hill Road. When these investors show up, the check sizes get very large, very fast.

curated_collections.name: The PayPal Mafia & Friends
curated_collections.tagline: Alumni of PayPal's founding team and the investors who run in their orbit. The original startup mafia — still the most influential network in tech.

curated_collections.name: The Marketplace Playbook
curated_collections.tagline: They understand liquidity, take rates, and chicken-and-egg problems better than anyone. The investors who wrote the book on two-sided marketplaces.

curated_collections.name: The Israel Connection
curated_collections.tagline: Investing in or from Israel's legendary startup ecosystem. Deep ties to Unit 8200 alumni, cybersecurity, and deep tech.

curated_collections.name: The European Vanguard
curated_collections.tagline: The investors building Europe's startup ecosystem from London, Berlin, Stockholm, and beyond. Proving you don't need Sand Hill Road.

curated_collections.name: The Biotech Believers
curated_collections.tagline: From CRISPR to synthetic biology to computational drug discovery. They bet on the biological century.

curated_collections.name: Founders Who Became VCs
curated_collections.tagline: They built companies first, then crossed to the other side of the table. They know what it's like to be in the founder's seat.

curated_collections.name: The Quiet Giants
curated_collections.tagline: They don't tweet, they don't podcast, they just write very large checks. The growth and late-stage investors who let returns do the talking.

curated_collections.name: The YC Network
curated_collections.tagline: Y Combinator partners and the investors who consistently back YC's best. When a YC company raises, these names are on the cap table.

curated_collections.name: The Cyber-Israel Nexus
curated_collections.tagline: Where Israel's cybersecurity expertise meets venture capital. These investors understand threat landscapes and have the 8200 alumni network on speed dial.

curated_collections.name: The Climate Vanguard
curated_collections.tagline: Betting the energy transition is the biggest investment opportunity of the century. These investors actually back the hard science, not just the marketing.

curated_collections.name: The Prolific Angels
curated_collections.tagline: Quantity AND quality. These investors have 40+ companies in their portfolios — they see more deals, write more checks, and build broader networks than anyone.

curated_collections.name: The Pear VC Partnership
curated_collections.tagline: Pejman Nozad and Mar Hershenson's pre-seed powerhouse. Stanford-rooted, technical-founder focused, with 19 shared investments and a deep Bay Area network.

curated_collections.name: Women in Venture
curated_collections.tagline: The women shaping venture capital — from pioneering GPs to the next generation of partners writing the biggest checks in tech.

curated_collections.name: The Dev Tools Obsessives
curated_collections.tagline: They back the picks and shovels every developer reaches for. Open source, infrastructure, and the building blocks of modern software.

curated_collections.name: The IPO Whisperers
curated_collections.tagline: Three or more portfolio companies that went public. These investors don't just pick winners — they pick companies that reach the finish line.

curated_collections.name: The Ron Conway Extended Family
curated_collections.tagline: Ron Conway's co-investment network — the investors who consistently show up on the same cap tables. When Ron says yes, these people are often right behind him.

curated_collections.name: The Entertainment Tech Investors
curated_collections.tagline: Gaming, streaming, social, and the creator economy. They understand attention, engagement, and what people do when they're not working.

curated_collections.name: The Moonshot Trifecta
curated_collections.tagline: Backed two or more of: OpenAI, Anthropic, SpaceX. They bet on the biggest, most ambitious ideas humanity has ever attempted.

curated_collections.name: The Pacific Northwest
curated_collections.tagline: Seattle, Portland, Vancouver — the other coast of West Coast tech. Amazon, Microsoft, and a growing startup ecosystem that doesn't need Sand Hill Road.

curated_collections.name: The Future of Work Investors
curated_collections.tagline: They backed the tools that replaced the office — Slack, Figma, Notion, Gusto, Rippling. Betting that how we work matters as much as what we build.

curated_collections.name: The YC Alumni Network
curated_collections.tagline: They built companies through Y Combinator, then crossed to the investor side. The YC network is the most powerful alumni network in startups — and they're proof.

curated_collections.name: The Decacorn Club
curated_collections.tagline: Backed three or more companies worth $10B+. When the biggest outcomes in tech history were being funded, these investors were in the room.

curated_collections.name: Authors Who Invest
curated_collections.tagline: They wrote the books founders actually read — then started writing checks. Venture capitalists who shaped startup thinking through bestsellers.

curated_collections.name: The Engineer-Investors
curated_collections.tagline: They wrote code before they wrote checks. Engineers and CTOs who became investors — they can read your architecture docs, not just your pitch deck.

curated_collections.name: The Proptech Investors
curated_collections.tagline: Real estate, construction, and the built environment. These investors believe the largest asset class in the world is still stuck in the 1990s.

curated_collections.name: The Food & Ag Tech Investors
curated_collections.tagline: From farm to fork — these investors back the companies reimagining how food is grown, distributed, and delivered.

curated_collections.name: The Sunbelt Migration
curated_collections.tagline: They left San Francisco for Miami, Austin, and beyond — and brought their deal flow with them. The investors proving venture capital doesn't need a 94025 zip code.

curated_collections.name: The Podcast VCs
curated_collections.tagline: They build in public — on microphones. Through All-In, Masters of Scale, No Priors, and more, they've made venture capital a spectator sport.

curated_collections.name: The Beltway Disruptors
curated_collections.tagline: DC-based investors who bet that the most regulated industries are the most ripe for disruption — defense, healthcare, govtech, and national security.

curated_collections.name: The Global South Scouts
curated_collections.tagline: They saw the next billion users before everyone else — investing in LatAm, India, and Southeast Asia when the rest of VC was stuck in SF.

curated_collections.name: The Goldman Pipeline
curated_collections.tagline: From 200 West Street to Sand Hill Road — Goldman Sachs alumni who turned investment banking training into venture capital careers.

curated_collections.name: The Defense Tech Vanguard
curated_collections.tagline: They bet on Anduril before it was cool — investors who saw defense tech as venture-scale before the geopolitical shift made it obvious.

curated_collections.name: The Persian Pipeline
curated_collections.tagline: Iranian-American investors who turned diaspora networks into dealflow — from Pejman's rug shop to Pear VC, this community punches far above its weight.

curated_collections.name: The Saffa Mafia
curated_collections.tagline: South Africans who quietly took over Silicon Valley — from running Sequoia to building PayPal to launching their own funds.

curated_collections.name: The Stripe Mafia
curated_collections.tagline: Early Stripe operators who took the lessons of building the world's most disciplined fintech and turned them into a venture playbook.

curated_collections.name: The Essayists
curated_collections.tagline: Their portfolios speak for themselves — but founders read their writing before pitching.
